recent_calls.summary: The user, Danielle, reported that her air conditioning system is completely unresponsive and requested service as soon as possible, preferably tomorrow. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that a representative will contact her on the next business day to confirm the appointment. No special instructions were provided.

The user called after hours and spoke with Evie, the after-hours assistant, who informed them that a live agent was unavailable but offered to take their information for a follow-up on the next business day. The call ended shortly after without the user providing further details.

Tracey Portell called to report an emergency leak from her air vents. The agent documented the issue, confirmed the details, and escalated the emergency for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

The user returned a call after receiving a message and requested a callback from the team the next business day to follow up on the original message. The agent confirmed the request and assured the user that the team would reach out tomorrow. The call ended with the user expressing thanks.

The user, Deanna, called to request a free upgrade for an HVAC system replacement at her service address in Fairview Heights, Illinois. The agent collected her contact details and noted that she wants a follow-up as soon as possible. The call ended with the agent confirming the request and promising a callback on the next business day.
The user expressed frustration immediately upon answering the call, and the agent acknowledged the user's frustration. The call ended shortly after without further conversation.
The user called to speak to a person named Mister Hello but the call ended abruptly shortly after the initial greeting without further conversation.
The call was very brief with the user greeting the agent, but no further conversation or assistance took place before the user hung up.

The user called after-hours seeking clarity on a dispatch related to repair work and upgrade options for a specific work order. The agent, Evie, explained that she could not access live service records but assured the user that the request for details and documentation would be forwarded to the team for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and the agent confirming the follow-up.

The user called to report that their technician did not call to reinstall the fan as expected. The agent apologized, documented the issue, and assured the user that the team would follow up first thing the next business day. The user confirmed that a follow-up first thing tomorrow was acceptable and ended the call politely.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent's initial greeting and request for the service address ZIP code. The user hung up without responding or engaging further.

The user, Hoffman, called to request a repair for a defective PTAC unit drip pan and hose leaking into the apartment below at 410 N Museid Ave, St. Louis. The agent confirmed the details, noted the user's preference for the earliest available appointment, and arranged for a technician to call first for access to the high-rise building. The user also expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer.
The user called to inquire if the location was open for making a delivery. The agent confirmed the hours and location, and offered further assistance.

The call was an inbound call where the agent attempted to engage the user by asking for a service address ZIP code, but the user identified herself as Sarah from AT&T and left a voicemail instead of responding to the agent's questions.

The user, Elesia, scheduled a cooling system service to replace a control board at a property she manages. The appointment was booked for Monday, July 13, between 8 AM and 12 PM. Despite the successful booking, the user expressed dissatisfaction with the dispatch fee and requested to speak with a human agent, leading to a call transfer.

The user called to cancel a service appointment scheduled for the next day. The agent confirmed the cancellation request would be passed on and that the user would receive a follow-up call to confirm. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and no further requests.
The user requested to speak to a human agent, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.
The agent greeted the user and immediately attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was completed successfully shortly after the call began.

The user called multiple times to schedule a follow-up appointment for a water heater plumbing repair due to a leak. The agent confirmed the service type and user's role as the property owner but the call ended due to inactivity before the appointment was scheduled.
The user called to speak with a live agent about their air conditioner. The initial agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request a repair for a broken wire under their mobile home affecting their air conditioner. The agent attempted to clarify the urgency and type of service needed but experienced connection issues and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user, Jacqueline, called to speak with someone regarding a recent survey and service. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user, Gabrielle, returned a call and was promptly transferred by the agent, Sophie, to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak to customer service station two, expressing frustration about AI handling calls. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The user, Wyatt, returned a call and was promptly transferred by the agent Sophie to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and requested to speak with CG. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent.

Cliff called to schedule a service appointment for his air conditioner, which has no power to the thermostat and won't turn on. The agent booked an appointment for the next day at 4 PM and attempted to gather feedback, but Cliff expressed dissatisfaction with the AI interaction and requested to speak with a live person. The call ended with the agent transferring Cliff to a live agent as requested.

The user called and mentioned something about the pool, but the conversation was very brief and the agent asked for the service address ZIP code to assist further. The call ended shortly after without the user providing the ZIP code.

The user from American Home Shield called Budget to follow up on a dispatch regarding a unit that was still not working after a technician's visit. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to ask several questions about the installation timeline, potential additional charges, and the start time after sending the signed contract. The agent provided detailed answers, clarifying that standard installations usually take one day with no extra charge for extended time unless special modifications are needed, and that work typically starts within three to seven business days after contract and payment. The user was satisfied and planned to send the contract soon.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Anne called to schedule a standard AC installation for a ninety percent unit following a recent inspection. The agent confirmed details and booked the installation for Thursday, July 23rd at 10 AM. The call ended with the agent requesting feedback and the user expressing thanks.

Gabrielle called to follow up on a previous service visit where a technician couldn't inspect a frozen unit. She requested to schedule a new inspection at a different address without incurring an additional service fee. The agent confirmed the details and assured that the team would follow up with her.
The user requested to speak with a manager, representative, or supervisor, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user called to schedule a routine plumbing service for a clogged water line between the sink and refrigerator. The agent collected the user's details and preferred appointment time, but the booking system returned an error indicating a conflict. The agent assured the user that a human agent would follow up to confirm the appointment.

The user called to inquire about service availability at their ZIP code 62088, but the agent informed them that service is not offered in that area. The user did not provide an alternative ZIP code, and the call ended with the agent thanking the user for calling.
The user requested to speak with a manager, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate person. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called and requested to speak with Siggi. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to another agent.
The user called but quickly realized they had the wrong number and ended the call shortly after. The agent responded politely and ended the call without further action.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with a representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and a follow-up prompt. The user did not respond or engage in the conversation.
The user requested to speak to someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user, Donald McNees, called to request an estimate for replacing an AC unit at his home. The agent booked an appointment for Friday, July 10th at 2 PM and clarified that the in-home quote and system evaluation are free of charge. The call ended successfully with the user satisfied and the appointment confirmed.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative as requested. The call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user called and requested to be connected to customer care. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.

The user called and requested to speak with a live representative. The agent acknowledged the request and offered to have someone follow up with the user directly. The call ended with the user mentioning a message that was left, but the call disconnected reaching voicemail.
The user called and requested to speak with Ryan. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate person.
